questions.question: What new machine payments protocol did Dan Romero launch in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero announced the launch of MPP (Machine Payments Protocol) co-authored with Stripe on March 18, 2026.

questions.question: Which company co-authored the Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) with Dan Romero?
questions.explanation: The MPP was co-authored with Stripe, as mentioned in Dan Romero's post on March 18, 2026.

questions.question: What issue did Jesse Pollak report regarding wallet balances on March 13, 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak noted that quite a few token and total balances were showing up inaccurately due to changes in where they fetch balances.

questions.question: On which date did the Tempo mainnet go live?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero announced Tempo mainnet was live on March 18, 2026.

questions.question: What specific edge case fix remained in progress for wallet balances according to Jesse Pollak's March 13 post?
options: Tokens with super low liquidity USDC pairs
questions.explanation: Jesse specified one known edge case fix in progress regarding tokens with super low liquidity USDC pairs not pricing correctly.

questions.question: Which tool did Brian Armstrong switch back to after using Warp?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted on March 7, 2026, that it felt good to be back to iTerm2 from Warp.

questions.question: What major sports event did Jesse Pollak enjoy watching in March 2026?
questions.explanation: On March 9, 2026, Jesse mentioned watching the cricket World Cup final with his kids.

questions.question: According to Dan Romero, which two entities were able to extend MPP in a matter of days?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero noted that Visa and Lightspark were each able to extend MPP in a matter of days.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak compared the future of AI agents and folding displays to which fictional object?
questions.explanation: Jesse suggested that LLMs and folding displays would make Tom Riddle's diary no longer fiction.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the interactive demo at mpp.dev?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero directed users to mpp.dev to check out the interactive demo for the Machine Payments Protocol.

questions.question: Brian Armstrong reported using up his weekly limit for which AI tool in one day?
questions.explanation: On March 8, 2026, Brian Armstrong mentioned using up all of his Claude Max 20x weekly limit in one day.

questions.question: What is the URL for the Neynar studio on the web as mentioned by Jesse Pollak?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak shared neynar.com/studio as the link for the Neynar studio on the web.

questions.question: Which developer built a Python port of the Farcaster hub-nodejs library?
questions.explanation: Mircea Pasoi (posted via Coinbase handle) built an incomplete Python port of @farcaster/hub-nodejs.

questions.question: What time window was specified for Farcaster database changes on February 5, 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak stated changes were happening between noon - 1PM PST on Feb 5.

questions.question: In Dan Romero's 'MPP myths' post, which blockchain is mentioned as having a draft extension for MPP?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero mentioned that there is a draft extension for Solana.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak mention happened at 12:00 PM UTC on February 24, 2026?
options: Shard 0 cutover to updated validator set
questions.explanation: Jesse expected instability when shard 0 cut over to the updated validator set at that time.

questions.question: Which game did Brian Armstrong mention having an AI workflow for 'OpenClaw' in his pocket?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted 'openclaw in my pocket lfg' on March 7, 2026.

questions.question: What was the result of the latency optimization for mcilroyc on Farcaster?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak shared that they achieved 50% less global p95 latency for mcilroyc.

questions.question: What was Brian Armstrong's feedback on 'gsd-2' after 3 days of use?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong stated: 'day 3 of using gsd-2... just not there yet. going back to vanilla claude code.'

questions.question: Which cricket team did Jesse Pollak's kids learn play in black colors?
questions.explanation: Jesse noted his kids now know New Zealand's colors are black.

questions.question: Base_official once asked for advice regarding which activity in March 2023?
questions.explanation: The Base official account asked gym goers for advice on staying consistent on March 25, 2023.

questions.question: What did Dan Romero describe as 'clutch' in his Balatro high score post?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero mentioned 'Invisible -> Brainstorm copy was clutch' during his flight on Sunday night.

questions.question: What was the first cast on Farcaster by the base_official account about?
options: Curiosity about where the road takes them
questions.explanation: On October 24, 2022, the first cast was 'First cast - can’t wait to see where this road takes me!'

questions.question: According to Jesse Pollak, what does 'grin' do at Neynar?
questions.explanation: Jesse mentioned '@grin is on a mission to send bugs right to jail!' as the Neynar way.

questions.question: What is the focus of the blog post Mircea Pasoi shared in November 2023?
options: Discovering he is on the autism spectrum
questions.explanation: Mircea shared a personal story about discovering he is on the autism spectrum.

questions.question: Which project did Brian Armstrong describe as a 'cool project' involving CorridorKey?
questions.explanation: Brian shared the nikopueringer/CorridorKey GitHub link on March 14, 2026.

questions.question: What feature did Jesse Pollak say was added to Neynar Studio in January 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse noted updates including securely configuring Environment Variables & secrets.

questions.question: Dan Romero mentioned MPP has been submitted to which standards organization?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero stated MPP has been submitted to the IETF as a web standard.

questions.question: What did Brian Armstrong ask 'docgpt' about on March 12, 2026?
options: Eating 20 beans a day and drinking coffee
questions.explanation: Brian joked about his diet of 20 beans and seven cups of coffee.

questions.question: According to Jesse Pollak, why was building Farcaster software 'as rewarding as a game'?
options: Coding agents shortened the effort-reward cycle
questions.explanation: Jesse stated coding agents shortened the time span of the effort-reward cycle.

questions.question: Which entity did Jesse Pollak credit for making Farcaster what it is today?
questions.explanation: Jesse gave props to dwr, v, and the team at Merkle for Farcaster's success.

questions.question: What specific feature update did Brian Armstrong say he is waiting for before saying 'wowow'?
questions.explanation: Brian posted on March 17: 'i'm only gonna say wowow when animated pfps come back'.

questions.question: What was the 'most joyous moment' of a weekend in March 2026 for Jesse Pollak?
options: Watching his kids be interested in cricket
questions.explanation: Jesse noted seeing his kids interested in watching and playing cricket was the most joyous moment.

questions.question: Which game's farms are Jesse Pollak's kids asking for?
questions.explanation: Jesse mentioned his kids are asking for their own /fruitling-valley farms.

questions.question: What did Dan Romero say about the cost of using MPP?
options: No inherent cost to using MPP
questions.explanation: Dan Romero's MPP myths post clarified there is 'No inherent cost to using MPP.'

questions.question: Jesse Pollak mentioned seeing issues on which network layer on February 24, 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse noted issues on the Farcaster network layer on Feb 24.

questions.question: What site did Brian Armstrong find for uploading files/sharing on March 12?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong asked how he just found out about catbox.moe.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak offer to give building rewards for on neynar.com/studio?
questions.explanation: On Feb 28, Jesse mentioned 'daily reward credits to build on neynar.com/studio'.

questions.question: Dan Romero mentioned MPP works with which payment methods out of the box?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero stated MPP is payment method agnostic and works with crypto and fiat.

questions.question: What is the name of the database Brian Armstrong called 'nuts' and wants to use in a side project?
questions.explanation: On Feb 28, Brian mentioned 'spacetimedb is nuts' and he would try it.

questions.question: Which entity did Jesse Pollak say was missing 'S tier shit posting' until Iceland showed up?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak shared a tweet about Iceland Cricket on Jan 28, 2026.

questions.question: What was the population growth percentage mentioned by Dan Romero on March 17, 2026?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero noted: 'And population only grew ~10%.'

questions.question: Brian Armstrong mentioned a daily find on GitHub called what?
questions.explanation: Brian shared the link to github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode on March 6.

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'tool call visibility' according to Jesse Pollak?
questions.explanation: Jesse stated 'tool call visibility helps us improve the bot faster' on Jan 21, 2026.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak stay up 'extremely late' building for a week in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse mentioned staying up late trying to build software to power Farcaster.

questions.question: Which humanoid robot did Jesse Pollak suggest giving an OpenClaw agent access to?
questions.explanation: Jesse asked if someone gave an OpenClaw agent access to a humanoid like the G1.

questions.question: Which of these was NOT one of the 4 payment methods MPP launched with on Day 1?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero listed Tempo, Stripe, Visa, and Lightspark as the Day 1 methods; PayPal was not mentioned.

questions.question: What specific latency metric did Jesse Pollak reference on January 30, 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse mentioned '50% less global p95 latency' for mcilroyc.

questions.question: According to Brian Armstrong, what 'sometimes gives you wings' but 'sometimes fucks you up'?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted on March 10 that ADHD is a powerful drug that sometimes gives you wings or fucks you up.

questions.question: What was the date of the post where base_official mentioned doubling revenue?
questions.explanation: The post from base_official regarding doubling revenue is dated 2024-03-15.
